title: Commit message missing rationale for OnceLock removal
description:
The commit message lists 'Remove chat_using() OnceLock; inline the two-string vec' as a what, but gives no why. The tradeoff being made is: simplicity (no OnceLock, no helper function, fewer lines) at the cost of two heap allocations per JMAP method call.

A reader doing archaeology via git log will not understand why the allocation was considered acceptable here, or whether it was an accidental omission of the OnceLock. The commit message (or a code comment near build_request) should explain the tradeoff — e.g. 'per-call allocation is acceptable for a network-bound path; the OnceLock added complexity without measurable benefit at this call volume.'

This is a documentation-of-intent issue, not a code change.
close_reason: Won't fix: cannot amend a pushed commit. The rationale is captured in the beads epic instead.
